title: feat: real-time local speech translation engine

dep_changes.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,60 @@
+[build-system]
+requires = ["setuptools>=68", "wheel"]
+build-backend = "setuptools.build_meta"
+
+[project]
+name = "realtime-speech-translator"
+version = "0.1.0"
+description = "Local-first real-time speech translation engine for live meetings (streaming ASR + context-aware translation + caption overlay)."
+readme = "README.md"
+requires-python = ">=3.10"
+license = { text = "MIT" }
+authors = [{ name = "Ejoutiya" }]
+keywords = ["speech", "translation", "asr", "whisper", "nllb", "captions", "realtime"]
+
+# Core dependencies are intentionally lightweight so the pipeline logic and the
+# full test-suite run without downloading multi-GB models. Heavy/optional
+# backends (audio capture, ASR, translation, GUI) live in extras below.
+dependencies = [
+    "numpy>=1.24",
+]
+
+[project.optional-dependencies]
+audio = ["sounddevice>=0.4.6"]
+asr = ["faster-whisper>=1.0.0"]
+# NLLB translation runs through CTranslate2 + a tokenizer; transformers provides
+# an easy fallback. Argos is a pure-offline alternative.
+translate = ["transformers>=4.38", "sentencepiece>=0.1.99", "ctranslate2>=4.0"]
+argos = ["argostranslate>=1.9"]
+ui = ["PyQt6>=6.5"]
+all = [
+    "sounddevice>=0.4.6",
+    "faster-whisper>=1.0.0",
+    "transformers>=4.38",
+    "sentencepiece>=0.1.99",
+    "ctranslate2>=4.0",
+    "PyQt6>=6.5",
+]
+dev = ["pytest>=7.4", "ruff>=0.5.0"]
+
+[project.scripts]
+rtst = "rtst.cli:main"
+
+[tool.setuptools.packages.find]
+where = ["src"]
+
+[tool.setuptools.package-data]
+rtst = ["py.typed"]
+
+[tool.ruff]
+line-length = 100
+target-version = "py310"
+src = ["src", "tests"]
+
+[tool.ruff.lint]
+select = ["E", "F", "I", "B", "UP", "W"]
+ignore = ["B008"]
+
+[tool.pytest.ini_options]
+testpaths = ["tests"]
+addopts = "-q"

+[metadata]
+license_file = LICENSE
+classifiers =
+    Development Status :: 5 - Production/Stable
+    Intended Audience :: Developers
+    Intended Audience :: System Administrators
+    License :: Eiffel Forum License (EFL)
+    License :: OSI Approved :: Eiffel Forum License
+    Operating System :: POSIX :: Linux
+    Programming Language :: Python :: 3.6
+    Programming Language :: Python :: 3.7
+    Programming Language :: Python :: 3.8
+    Topic :: Communications :: Chat :: Internet Relay Chat
+[options]
+python_requires = >=3.6
+[options.entry_points]
+sopel.plugins =
+    adminlist   = MirahezeBots.plugins.adminlist
+    channelmgnt = MirahezeBots.plugins.channelmgnt
+    joinall     = MirahezeBots.plugins.joinall
+    miraheze    = MirahezeBots.plugins.miraheze
+    phab        = MirahezeBots.plugins.phab
+    pingpong    = MirahezeBots.plugins.pingpong
+    responses   = MirahezeBots.plugins.responses
+    rss         = MirahezeBots.plugins.rss
+    shortlinks  = MirahezeBots.plugins.shortlinks
+    status      = MirahezeBots.plugins.status
+    welcome     = MirahezeBots.plugins.welcome
+    goofy       = MirahezeBots.plugins.goofy
+console_scripts =
+    sopel-dbclean = MirahezeBots.dbclean:main

@@ -0,0 +1,33 @@
+from __future__ import print_function
+import os
+import sys
+from setuptools import setup, find_packages
+
+with open('README.md') as readme_file:
+    readme = readme_file.read()
+
+with open('CHANGELOG.md') as history_file:
+    history = history_file.read()
+with open('requirements.txt') as requirements_file:
+    requirements = [req for req in requirements_file.readlines()]
+
+with open('dev-requirements.txt') as dev_requirements_file:
+    dev_requirements = [req for req in dev_requirements_file.readlines()]
+
+
+setup(
+    name='MirahezeBot_Plugins',
+    version='8.0.0',
+    description='Sopel Plugins for Miraheze Bots',
+    long_description=readme + '\n\n' + history,
+    long_description_content_type='text/markdown',  # This is important!
+    author='MirahezeBot Contributors',
+    author_email='redacted@example.invalid',
+    url='https://github.com/MirahezeBots/MirahezeBots',
+    packages=find_packages('.'),
+    include_package_data=True,
+    install_requires=requirements,
+    tests_require=dev_requirements,
+    test_suite='tests',
+    license='Eiffel Forum License, version 2',
+)

title: approve-watch: cursor-agent watcher + Textual approval dashboard

dep_changes.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,30 @@
+[build-system]
+requires = ["hatchling"]
+build-backend = "hatchling.build"
+
+[project]
+name = "approve-watch"
+version = "0.1.0"
+description = "Watch cursor-agent / cmux panes, log command-approval prompts to SQLite, and approve them via a Textual dashboard."
+requires-python = ">=3.11"
+dependencies = [
+    "textual>=0.80",
+    "textual-plotext>=0.2.1",
+    "click>=8.1",
+]
+
+[project.optional-dependencies]
+dev = [
+    "pytest>=8.0",
+    "pytest-asyncio>=0.23",
+]
+
+[project.scripts]
+approve-watch = "approve_watch.cli:main"
+
+[tool.hatch.build.targets.wheel]
+packages = ["src/approve_watch"]
+
+[tool.pytest.ini_options]
+asyncio_mode = "auto"
+testpaths = ["tests"]

title: feat: migrate managed reviews to multi-agent coordinator (5 sessions → 1)

dep_changes.patch:
@@ -192,12 +192,12 @@ def main():
     print("[2] Fetching agent list...")
     agents_by_name = list_agents()
 
-    # 3. Specialist agents. No orchestrator — the Python driver in review.py
-    # is the orchestrator. Anthropic's parallel-sub-agents feature
-    # (callable_agents) is gated behind the multiagent Research Preview, so
-    # we fan out client-side from review.py via asyncio.gather instead.
+    # 3. Specialist agents. They become `callable_agents` of the
+    # coordinator below — the coordinator dispatches them as sub-agents
+    # within a single session, replacing the prior client-side
+    # asyncio.gather over 5 separate sessions.
     print("[3] Specialist agents")
-    synced = []
+    synced: dict[str, dict] = {}
     for name in SUB_AGENTS:
         prompt_file = AGENTS_DIR / f"{name}.md"
         if not prompt_file.exists():
@@ -220,9 +220,46 @@ def main():
             existing=agents_by_name.get(f"air-{name}"),
             model=model,
         )
-        synced.append(agent)
+        synced[name] = agent
+
+    # 4. Coordinator agent. Multi-agent orchestrator that calls all 5
+    # specialists as sub-agents via callable_agents. Skipped if
+    # coordinator.md is absent (lets older trees still sync specialists
+    # without erroring).
+    coordinator_file = AGENTS_DIR / "coordinator.md"
+    coordinator: dict | None = None
+    if coordinator_file.exists():
+        print("[4] Coordinator agent (multi-agent dispatcher)")
+        if len(synced) < len(SUB_AGENTS):
+            print(
+                f"  air-coordinator: SKIPPED — only {len(synced)}/{len(SUB_AGENTS)} "
+                f"sub-agents synced; coordinator needs all 5 to declare callable_agents",
+                file=sys.stderr,
+            )
+        else:
+            system = read_prompt(coordinator_file)
+            tools = parse_agent_tools(coordinator_file)
+            model = parse_agent_model(coordinator_file, default=MODEL_ALIASES["sonnet"])
+            tool_configs = [{"name": t, "enabled": True} for t in tools]
+            callable_agents = [
+                {"type": "agent", "id": synced[n]["id"], "version": synced[n]["version"]}
+                for n in SUB_AGENTS
+            ]
+            coordinator = create_or_update_agent(
+                name="air-coordinator",
+                system=system,
+                tools=[{
+                    "type": "agent_toolset_20260401",
+                    "default_config": {"enabled": False},
+                    "configs": tool_configs,
+                }],
+                existing=agents_by_name.get("air-coordinator"),
+                callable_agents=callable_agents,
+                model=model,
+            )
 
-    print(f"\nDone. {len(synced)} specialist agents synced. review.py orchestrates client-side.")
+    coord_status = "+ coordinator" if coordinator else "(coordinator absent)"
+    print(f"\nDone. {len(synced)} specialist agents synced {coord_status}.")
 
 
 if __name__ == "__main__":

+[project]
+name = "auto-prd"
+version = "0.1.0"
+description = "Automated PRD-to-PR pipeline for AI-assisted development"
+readme = "../../README.md"
+requires-python = ">=3.10"
+license = {text = "MIT"}
+authors = [
+    {name = "Simo Kiihamaki"}
+]
+keywords = ["automation", "prd", "pull-request", "ai", "development"]
+classifiers = [
+    "Development Status :: 4 - Beta",
+    "Intended Audience :: Developers",
+    "License :: OSI Approved :: MIT License",
+    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3",
+    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.10",
+    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.11",
+    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.12",
+    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.13",
+]
+
+[project.scripts]
+auto-prd = "auto_prd.cli:main"
+
+[build-system]
+requires = ["setuptools>=61.0"]
+build-backend = "setuptools.build_meta"
+
+[tool.setuptools.packages.find]
+where = [".."]
+include = ["auto_prd*"]
+
+[tool.ruff]
+line-length = 88
+target-version = "py310"
+
+[tool.ruff.lint]
+select = ["E", "F", "W", "I", "B", "C4", "UP", "ARG", "SIM"]
+
+[tool.mypy]
+python_version = "3.10"
+warn_return_any = true
+warn_unused_configs = true

+[project]
+name = "auto-prd"
+version = "0.1.0"
+description = "Automated PRD-to-PR pipeline for AI-assisted development"
+readme = "../../README.md"
+requires-python = ">=3.10"
+license = {text = "MIT"}
+authors = [
+    {name = "Simo Kiihamaki"}
+]
+keywords = ["automation", "prd", "pull-request", "ai", "development"]
+classifiers = [
+    "Development Status :: 4 - Beta",
+    "Intended Audience :: Developers",
+    "License :: OSI Approved :: MIT License",
+    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3",
+    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.10",
+    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.11",
+    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.12",
+    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.13",
+]
+
+[project.scripts]
+auto-prd = "auto_prd.cli:main"
+
+[build-system]
+requires = ["setuptools>=61.0"]
+build-backend = "setuptools.build_meta"
+
+[tool.setuptools.packages.find]
+where = [".."]
+include = ["auto_prd*"]
+
+[tool.ruff]
+line-length = 88
+target-version = "py310"
+
+[tool.ruff.lint]
+select = ["E", "F", "W", "I", "B", "C4", "UP", "ARG", "SIM"]
+
+[tool.mypy]
+python_version = "3.10"
+warn_return_any = true
+warn_unused_configs = true

+[build-system]

+[project]
+name = "lazypandas"
+version = "0.2.0"
+description = "A collection of data wrangling helper methods for pandas."
+readme = "README.md"
+license = { file = "LICENSE" }
+authors = [{ name = "Vinny Pasceri", email = "redacted@example.invalid" }]
+requires-python = ">=3.10"
+keywords = ["pandas", "dataframe", "csv", "helpers"]

+  "Framework :: Jupyter",
+  "Intended Audience :: Developers",
+  "License :: OSI Approved :: MIT License",

+  "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.10",
+  "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.11",

+  "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.13",

+dependencies = [

+

+

+testpaths = ["tests"]
+addopts = "-ra"
